content_blocks.text:
Make a Connection
15 networking and threads
Connect with the outside world. Your Java program can reach out and touch a 
program on another machine. It’5 easy. All the low-level networking details are taken care of 
by classes in the java.net library. One of Java’5 big benefits is that sending and receiving data 
over a network is just 1/0 with a slightly different connection stream at the end of the chain. If 
you’ve got a BufferedReader, you can read. And the BufferedReader couldn’t care less if the data 
came out of a file or flew down an ethernet cable. In this chapter we’11 connect to the outside 
world with sockets. We’11 make client sockets. We’11 make server sockets. We’11 make clients and 
servers. And we’11 make them talk to each other. Before the chapter’5 done, you’11 have a fully-
functional, multithreaded chat client. Did we just say multithreaded? Yes, now you will learn 
the secret of how to talk to Bob while simultaneously listening to Suzy.
this is a new chapter
471
